THE SCOPE OF DOCUMENTS TO BE PRODUCED IS LIMITED TO 5 YEARS AS TO THE ENTIRE SUBPOENA, EXCEPT THAT DOCUMENTS RELATING TO CORRESPONDENCE, COMMUNICATIONS AND FEEDBACK MAY REQUIRE FURTHER LIMITATIONS. THE RULING IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO FURTHER MOTION TO QUASH, IN WHICH CASE THE PLAINTIFF AGREES TO PROVIDE A PRIVILEGE LOG. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING THE [PROPOSED] ORDER AND IF THERE IS NO AGREEMENT, EACH SIDE WILL SUBMIT COMPETING [PROPOSED] ORDERS VIA EMAIL AND IN WORD (.DOC) FORMAT DIRECTLY TO JUDGE PRO TEM STEIN. calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-24-2018 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-27-2017 Set For Trial On Oct-15-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion In Support Of Plaintiffs Motion To Quash Defendant Arrowhead General Insurance Agency, Inc.S Subpoenas And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-17-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Aaron Minnis of the law offices of Minnis & Smallets LLP (415-551-0885) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Victoria L. Tallman of the law offices of Davis Wright Tremaine LLP (415-276-6523) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

Both sdies stipulate to judge pro tem.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF CAROLINE BARWICK'S Motion In Support Of Plaintiffs Motion To Quash Defendant Arrowhead General Insurance Agency, Inc.S Subpoenas And Request For Sanctions is DENIED as moot. The defendant has withdrawn its subpoena. Both parties request for sanctions are denied. The prevailing party shall submit via email, directly to Judge Pro Tem Stein a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven B. Stein; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion In Support Of Motion To Quash Defendant Arrowhead General Insurance Agency, Inc.S Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Ace Westchester And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-17-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Aaron Minnis of the law offices of Minnis & Smallets LLP (415-551-0885) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Victoria L. Tallman of the law offices of Davis Wright Tremaine LLP (415-276-6523) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

Both sdies stipulate to judge pro tem.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF CAROLINE BARWICK'S Motion In Support Of Motion To Quash Defendant Arrowhead General Insurance Agency, Inc.S Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Ace Westchester And Request For Sanctions is DENIED on procedural grounds. The scope of documents to be produced is limited to 5 years as to the entire subpoena, except that documents relating to correspondence, communications and feedback may require further limitations. The ruling is without prejudice to further motion to quash, in which case the plaintiff agrees to provide a privilege log. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312. The parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding the [proposed] order and if there is no agreement, each side will submit competing [proposed] orders via email and in Word (.doc) format directly to Judge Pro Tem Stein.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven B. Stein; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion In Support Of Plaintiffs Motion To Quash Defendant Arrowhead General Insurance Agency, Inc.S Subpoenas And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-21-2017 Continued To Oct-17-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF CAROLINE BARWICK's Motion To Quash Defendant Arrowhead General Insurance Agency, Inc.'s Subpoenas And Request For Sanctions is continued to October 17, 2017. The Court notes the parties agree defendant's subpoenas of business records were withdrawn prior to any production of plaintiff's employment records and the remaining issue here is each party's request for sanctions. The Court also notes plaintiff has recently filed another motion to quash the same subpoena, again seeking sanctions, which will be heard on October 17, 2017 at 9 a.m. in the Discovery department. Therefore this motion is continued to October 17, 2017 at 9 a.m., to allow the Court to hear the two motions together at a time when all briefing has been completed. Judge Pro Tem: Melinda Derish; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
